item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, this is a good opportunity to ask: am I supposed/allowed to tag my own articles?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huh… strange! Doesn't seem to be showing up on the list of untagged pages for some reason. I'll poke someone about it.

ETA: looks like it has a hidden image tag, hence why it is technically not "untagged".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Echoing the above that there are a lot of disparate elements here which don't quite mesh with each other. There's good stuff here and I think if you went through this and whittled down a lot of the extra purely atmospheric or ancillary or what-have-you details it would make your central story easier to engage with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey there, thanks for your feedback!

I agree with you on some of the blindspots in this piece. Definitely hoping to make some revisions to the piece soon to address them. Thanks for your honesty and patience in the meantime!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Unfortunately, I can't upvote this. I figured the stain was related to the baby, and the rest of the work just didn't develop enough on it afterwards. There were a lot of distracting arcs, like the Dr. Wu being weirdly aggressive with testing (to no apparent benefit) and the RTF captain resisting it that did not contribute to the overall aesthetic.

I appreciate what's being done here, but I think it significantly lacks narrative cohesion and emotional investment. No vote, leaning towards downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for the compliment. I was worried that the slow reveal was too slow, but I'm glad it paid off for you in the end and created an emotional reaction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not gonna lie, that last log got to me a bit. The rest of it was really great too though, and the slow realization that this skip wasn't just some creepy stain actually made my stomach sink. I'm by no means a qualified critic, but for a first SCP, this leaves a pretty damn good first impression.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: hey thanks for the comments! You're right! I'll try and clear up his comment when I have time. Thanks for spotting it!

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Noted! Will try to find alternatives or contact the author for permission directly.

item.title: Re: Thank you!
item.content:encoded:
Hey thanks for your comment!

Hmm I guess I was going for the idea that they're still human at the end of the day, and should be portrayed and treated as such. Another way to read it is the opposite: the RTF guy was just concerned about his asset being damaged.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
All Good!
Click To Hide

The images you have sourced are under the CC BY-NC-SA 2.0 license and are therefore not compliant with the SCP Wiki's CC-BY-SA 3.0 license. As such, the images have been removed. Make sure to follow our Image Use Policy when picking any future images to make sure the license is compatible.

Stay away from NC & ND images, and make sure you read the Image Use Policy. Wikimedia Commons is a good source for compliant images, I'd check there.

Images have been changed to alternatives, _cc tag has been applied.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i'm, kinda really conflicted on this skip

the idea is really good and the way it's done is excellent except it feels, overly long? i'm not sure the second collapsible adds much other than a more sinister feel and it'd be the main thing to cut if you were going to? but it might just be that i prefer shorter stuff so *shrugs*

my other thing is that Dr. Wu's question about the first trimester check-up feels like it comes out of nowhere? like my first read through it felt really jarring and brought me right out of the fiction; like, it's necessary foreshadowing but it feels,,,like it comes too out of left-field? perhaps a softer start to the questioning on this like e.g, Dr. Wu asking how they're doing or something and them not mentioning the pregnancy and stuff?

item.title: Re: Thank you!
item.content:encoded: Pretty good SCP, just one thing kind of confused me: Why does the RTF guy care about the safety of the D-Class's safety so much? I thought D-Class were pretty much expendable to the Foundation.

item.title: Thank you!
item.content:encoded:
EDIT: A big thank you to all those who have voted positively so far! After more feedback from * pxdnbluesoul, some significant revisions to the piece include removing the distracting side-narratives that don't serve the main purpose of the story. Hopefully this time Dr. Wu doesn't come off as too big of an ass-hole either :p

***

Want to thank all the lovely (and patient) people for helping me out with the drafting of this:

  • Uncle Nicolini, Geckoguy2704, N_Aepic_Fael and minmin for the initial idea-bouncing and eventual greenlights.
  • Leveritas, Crashington, DrChandra and RockTeethMothEyes for really nit-picking on tone, dialogue, structure and all sorts of universe-ending plot-holes.

Your willingness to guide us newbies is much appreciated!

EDIT to image sources:

All images under Creative Commons CC0 (no copyright).

  • https://www.publicdomainpictures.net/en/view-image.php?image=21550&picture=mold-wall
  • https://pxhere.com/en/photo/692868
  • https://pxhere.com/en/photo/682859
